steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: The passage places Drelichman and Voth's research alongside Brewer's earlier work on European early modern state finance, and notes their unique contribution — reconstructing the earliest extant fiscal records — which adds new texture to that prior body of research.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D captures this relationship: D&V build on earlier work (like Brewer's) and add nuance through their reconstruction of fiscal records.

Choice A overstates the contrast with Brewer; choice B introduces specific causal claims about cash shortages that the passage doesn't establish as the main point; choice C ignores the connection to prior scholarship that the passage explicitly draws.

The answer is D.
